name: Stuffed Soft-Shell Crawfish
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the top shell from each crawfish and the two calcium deposits (hard, stonelike substances above the eyes); discard.
2. Replace the shell with Crawfish Stuffing (stuffing can be applied easily using a pastry bag).
3. Dip crawfish first in buttermilk, then flour; fry until golden brown.
4. Cover with Hollandaise sauce and serve.
CRAWFISH STUFFING
1. Melt butter in a large saucepan; add flour and stir over heat until light brown.
2. Add onion and garlic; sauté.
3. Remove from heat; add parsley, seasonings, egg, chopped bread, and crawfish tails and stir until mixed.
HOLLANDAISE SAUCE
1. Combine all ingredients except butter in the top of a double boiler.
2. Heat water in the bottom of the double boiler to boiling point.
3. With whisk, mix egg mixture over hot water until the egg is creamy and lemon-colored (will thicken to the consistency of soft pudding).
4. Remove from heat and continue to whisk while slowly adding butter.
